zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Simcotts於1526年出生在英國林可爾史爾吉爾頓。她於大約1550年與Robert Smith Sr.在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 基路士 維特 可利普萊加特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1624年在英國亨廷格多史爾享年98歲。

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle

The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Simcotts was born in 1526, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England. She married Robert Smith Sr. about 1550, in St Giles without Cripplegate, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1624, in Huntingdonshire, England, at the age of 98.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Simcottsは1526年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアキルトンで生まれた。約1550年にイギリスイギリスロンドンスト ギレス ウィソウト クリップレガトでRobert Smith Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1624年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアで98歳で死去した。

ko.St Giles without Cripplegate, London, En: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Simcotts(은)는 1526년 영국 링코실르 커턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트에서 Robert Smith Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1624년 영국 헌틴그도실르에서 98세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Simcotts родилась в 1526 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Smith Sr. около 1550 г. в местности ст джилесь витоут крипплегэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1624 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия в возрасте 98 лет.
